action: Model contradictions and state-layer divergence as linked provenance records rather than overwriting earlier observations.

progress.first_subject: Canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE baseline versus later append-only continuity updates
progress.resolution: Reviewed baseline and append-only candidate updates are distinct state layers; both are preserved until explicit reviewed compaction.

claim_states.observed: The canonical queue retains M-001 while later append-only continuity commits exist.
claim_states.inferred: Single-source readers can misread either layer as complete state.
claim_states.proposed: Future contradictions should use the same linked-record schema.
claim_states.unresolved: The canonical queue still requires explicit reviewed compaction before it represents later candidate state.

privacy_boundary: Public repository files and commit metadata only; private chat, health information, credentials, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads excluded.
handoff: Next continuity slice should index the decision that canonical queue compaction is review-gated, then add a deterministic validator that rejects contradiction records lacking two independently referenced observations or a preservation rule.
